sourceRef.citation: Wadler 1998, mailing-list discussion; Oliveira & Cook 2012 for object algebras

statement.en: Expression Problem (Wadler 1998): can one design a programming language construct that allows adding both new data variants and new operations to an existing module without modifying the module, while preserving static type safety and without runtime overhead?

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Data-op duality is structurally dual; current solutions pick one side
reiTyping.rationale: Partial solutions (typeclasses, tagless-final, object algebras) exist but tradeoffs remain

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Known partial techniques; ultimate canonical solution open
